findings.issue: The lexical FTS core (searchAyahs → monlite's FTS5, unicode61 tokenizer, no normalize/tokenize option set) matches whole word-tokens only, but Arabic proclitics (و/ف/ب/ل/ك and the attached ال-prefix combos) glue onto the following word with no space in Uthmani/clean text. A defenite-noun query like «الصبر» therefore almost never appears as its own token — every real occurrence is «بالصبر»/«فالصبر»/«واصبر» etc., which FTS5 tokenizes as one different token.
findings.evidence: Direct FTS5 MATCH test on quran-app.db: query «الصبر» → 0 rows (LIKE substring on the same field → 4 rows, e.g. a52 'واستعينوا بالصبر...', a160 'استعينوا بالصبر...' — literal 'الصبر' never occurs unprefixed). query «الشكر» → 0 rows both via MATCH and LIKE (the bare definite form never occurs at all; only 'اشكروا/يشكرون/شكور/الشاكرين' etc.). ThematicThread.tsx:54 `searchAyahs(query)` is exactly this call.

findings.issue: The 'rare root gets its full occurrence list' fallback (meant to compensate for FTS misses) is gated by `rvLocs.length <= 50 ? rvLocs : []` (ThematicThread.tsx:69) — a binary cliff with no scaling. Every one of the 5 requested probe roots, and every flagship SUGGESTIONS chip on the page itself, has 69–339 occurrences, so this fallback fires for NONE of them. Root grounding only works for genuinely rare/hapax words (the وسوس example used to justify the fix, 5 occurrences) — i.e. exactly the opposite of the page's own advertised use case (docs/mechanism-roadmap.md:30 names «الصبر» as the flagship generalization example).
findings.evidence: Live simulation against quran-app.db + quran-embeddings.bin (real Gemini query embeddings) for the owner's 5 probes: root صبر occurrences=103 → gated off (0 added); رزق=123 → gated off; توب=87 → gated off; شكر=75 → gated off; نفق=111 → gated off. All 5 of the SUGGESTIONS chips' underlying roots (رحم=339, صبر=103, شكر=75, توب=87, …) exceed 50.

findings.issue: Combined effect of the two findings above: for all 5 owner-specified probe queries the displayed 'thread' captures only a small fraction of the concept's real footprint in the mushaf — this is the empirical proof that the 'tightening' correction over-corrected into a severe recall failure, which is exactly what the owner was unsure about.
findings.evidence: Recall = (ground-truth root-occurrence verses that appear anywhere in the final displayed thread) / (total root-occurrence verses), measured end-to-end (FTS + root-fallback + top-200 semantic + 12-item halo, real embeddings): الصبر 4/93 = 4%; الرزق 24/109 = 22%; التوبة 16/69 = 23%; الشكر 11/69 = 16%; النفاق 11/86 = 13%. Total displayed thread sizes were only 4, 25, 16, 12, 13 verses respectively — «الصبر» (patience), a concept occurring ~100 times in the Quran and the doc's own headline demo example, shows only 4 verses to the user.

findings.issue: fuzzyRoots (letter edit-distance over whole 1651-root list) is applied to the raw query string with no guard for multi-word phrases. For a phrase, edit distance against a single 3-4 letter root is meaningless, yet the code always accepts 'best of 1651' with no absolute-distance sanity floor (only a relative best+0.3 window) — so it can silently inject an unrelated root's full occurrence list into the lexical core, mislabeled as 'precise/rare'.
findings.evidence: Query «التوبة والمغفرة» (a SUGGESTIONS chip) → best root match returned is «تبر» (dist=8.5, occurrences=6, so it passes the ≤50 gate) instead of «توب». Its 4 locations (7:139, 17:7, 25:39, 71:28 — all about تتبير/destruction, e.g. 'وليتبروا ما علوا تتبيرا') get added to the map as `lex-root` (implying exact, rarity-tagged grounding) even though they are about ruin, not repentance. Query «الصبر عند الشدّة» similarly best-matches root «بعد» (dist=9.5) — nonsense, only saved from polluting the result here because بعد's occurrence count (235) happens to exceed the 50 gate.

findings.issue: The semantic-halo bound (`score < top - 0.05` OR count ≥ 12`, ThematicThread.tsx:76-80) is a fixed absolute cosine-gap magic number, not adaptive to the actual score-curve shape. Because Quranic-verse embeddings cluster tightly (the exact 'fuzziness' problem the comment itself describes), the 0.05 gap essentially never triggers for common/broad concepts — the score decays by only ~0.02–0.06 across the whole top-20, so the rule degenerates to 'always take the hard-coded top-12', with no real coherence check. It only produced a genuinely adaptive cutoff for one of the 5 probes (الصبر, cut to 4 because of one real early gap).
findings.evidence: Top-20 score sequences from live runs — الرزق: 0.750,0.745,0.741,...0.713 (halo hit the 12-cap, never the gap); التوبة: 0.758→0.706 across 20 (12-cap hit); الشكر: 0.727,0.706,0.689...0.673 (12-cap hit); النفاق: 0.719...0.686 (12-cap hit). Only الصبر (0.785→0.753→0.744→0.739→0.733, i.e. a real 0.05 drop after rank 4) triggered the gap rule before the cap. 4 of 5 probes are just 'top-12', unrelated to any coherence property of the query.

findings.issue: The UI gives the user no way to tell why a verse is in the thread (literal FTS hit, full-root occurrence, or semantic-only halo pick) — it only shows a bare percentage or a bullet '•' (ThematicThread.tsx:139), and that percentage is itself accidental: any lexical hit that also happens to land in the top-200 semantic list gets a %, others get '•', with no legend explaining the difference. Combined with finding 2, most concept threads today are silently 100% semantic-halo (e.g. الصبر, الشكر) while looking identical in the UI to a thread with real lexical grounding.
findings.evidence: ThematicThread.tsx:138-140 renders only `{v.p > 0 ? ...% : "•"}`; no `src`/provenance field exists on the `TV` interface (line 18) at all. Live run for «الشكر»: 12/12 displayed verses are `halo`-sourced (0 lex-fts, 0 lex-root) yet render identically to a lexically-grounded thread.

how_it_works:
ThematicThread.tsx (/khayt) builds a 'thread' for a typed concept in three additive layers, merged into a Map keyed by verse location (dedup) then re-sorted into mushaf order:

1. Lexical core, layer A — `searchAyahs(query)` (db.ts:289) calls monlite's FTS5 `search()` against `ayahs_fts` (built by `fts({ ayahs: ["textClean"] })` in both convert-to-app-db.mjs and db.ts, with NO `tokenize`/`normalize` option). This is a straight FTS5 MATCH with the default `unicode61` tokenizer on `textClean` (diacritics already stripped at build time, but word-tokenized as-is — attached proclitics ب/و/ف/ك/ل/الـ are NOT split off).

2. Lexical core, layer B — `fuzzyRoots(query, 1)` (db.ts:221) finds the single letter-closest root (weak-letter-aware edit distance over all 1651 roots) and, if that root's full location list (deduped to verse level) has ≤50 entries, adds every one of those verses to the map (`rootLocs.length <= 50 ? rvLocs : []`, ThematicThread.tsx:69). Meant to recover morphological forms FTS misses for rare/specific words.

3. Semantic halo — `meaningSearch(query, 200)` (semantic.ts) embeds the query (Gemini `gemini-embedding-001`, RETRIEVAL_QUERY task type, via `/api/embed` or a user key) and does a raw brute-force cosine scan against the 6,236 int8-quantized ayah vectors shipped in `quran-embeddings.bin`, returning the top 200 by cosine score (NOT the previous `centroidProximity` technique — that function is now dead code, kept only for reference). ThematicThread then walks this list in descending-score order and adds up to 12 NEW (not already in the map) verses, stopping early if a verse's score falls more than 0.05 below the top score (ThematicThread.tsx:75-80).

The per-surah 'heat' strip takes, per sūra, the max of each included verse's semantic score (`v.p`, defaulting to a flat 0.55 for verses with no semantic score at all), min-max-normalized into a 0.3–1.0 range. The verse list below shows all deduped verses in mushaf order with `v.p` as a % or '•' if zero.

proposals.title: Switch the ayahs FTS index to a proclitic-tolerant tokenizer (trigram or a custom Arabic-prefix-stripping normalize)
proposals.rationale: This is the root cause: monlite/fts's own SearchIndexOptions.tokenize docs literally describe this exact failure ('trigram — substring matching, robust for Arabic/CJK, where word boundaries and the definite article ال defeat the default unicode61'). Switching `fts({ ayahs: [...] })` to `{ fields: ["textClean"], tokenize: "trigram" }` (or writing a normalize function that strips leading و/ف/ب/ك/ل/ال before indexing AND before querying) would make «الصبر» actually match 'بالصبر/فالصبر/والصبر' etc. — recovering the literal-occurrence grounding that's supposed to be the thread's 'core'. Needs a full rebuild of quran-app.db (convert-to-app-db.mjs) plus the matching client-side plugin config in db.ts, then re-verify FTS counts against root occurrence counts.

proposals.title: Stop discarding root grounding for common concepts — replace the binary ≤50 gate with always-include + UI pagination/collapse
proposals.rationale: The occurrence count of a root is ground truth about the concept's real footprint, not noise to filter out; today the gate makes the app show a 4-verse thread for a 103-occurrence concept (الصبر) — the opposite of what a 'trace this meaning across the whole mushaf' tool should do. Once the FTS fix above is in place, root-derived verses become mostly redundant with FTS hits anyway (safety net for the remainder); the display-size problem (too many verses on screen) should be solved at the UI layer (paginate / 'show all N occurrences' / group by sūra) rather than by silently dropping data from the underlying computation.

proposals.title: Guard fuzzyRoots against multi-word phrase queries and add an absolute-distance floor
proposals.rationale: Letter edit-distance against single roots is meaningless for phrase input; today it can (and did, for «التوبة والمغفرة») silently pick a totally unrelated root (تبر, dist=8.5) and label its verses as precise/rare lexical grounding. Fix: skip the root-fallback entirely when the trimmed query contains whitespace (treat it as a concept phrase, not a word/root lookup), and/or reject any 'best' match whose absolute dist exceeds a small ceiling (e.g. 2) regardless of the relative best+0.3 window.

proposals.title: Replace the fixed 0.05 cosine-gap halo cutoff with an adaptive elbow/percentile rule
proposals.rationale: Measured score curves show the fixed gap almost never fires for common concepts (4 of 5 probes just hit the hard 12-cap with no real elbow), so today's 'adaptive threshold' is in practice just 'take the top 12' — not adaptive at all, and not something the owner can currently distinguish from a genuinely tight halo (e.g. الصبر's real 4-item cutoff) just by looking at the code. A principled version: compute the score distribution over the full top-200 (mean/std or successive-gap array), and cut at the first gap that is a statistical outlier (e.g. z-score > 1.5 of the gap sequence) or at a percentile of the full 6,236-verse score distribution, instead of an absolute constant relative to rank-1 only. Optionally cross-check with mutual-kNN (only keep a halo verse if the seed query's top hit is itself among that verse's own nearest neighbors) to filter out generically 'Quranic-register-similar' verses.

proposals.title: Show each verse's provenance in the UI (لفظ صريح / امتداد الجذر / صدى دلالي)
proposals.rationale: Users currently cannot tell why a verse is included, and — given finding 2 — most concept threads today are silently 100% semantic-halo while looking identical to a lexically-grounded one. Tag each TV with its source (already computed as `src` internally in this review's simulation) and render a small badge/color instead of a bare % or bullet, so the owner's own quality checks (and end users) can see at a glance whether a thread is lexically solid or mostly semantic guesswork.

verdict: The owner's instinct was right to be unsure: the 'tightening' fix solved the over-fetching problem it targeted (pure-embedding fuzziness) but, in doing so, broke lexical grounding for almost every real-world query. Two independent, compounding bugs are responsible — an un-stemmed FTS5 tokenizer that Arabic's attached proclitics (و/ف/ب/ك/ل + ال) structurally defeat, and a root-occurrence gate (≤50) that excludes every substantive concept, including all ten of the page's own suggestion chips. Verified end-to-end against the real database and real Gemini embeddings, the result for the owner's 5 probe words is a thread that captures only 4–23% of each concept's actual occurrences in the mushaf — for «الصبر», the app's own flagship demonstration example (named in docs/mechanism-roadmap.md), it shows just 4 of ~93 real occurrences. The semantic halo itself is reasonably precise (spot-checked verses are genuinely on-topic) but its 'adaptive' 0.05-gap rule is in practice just a hard top-12 cap for common concepts, and a phrase-query edge case (fuzzyRoots given multi-word input) demonstrably injects an unrelated root's verses mislabeled as precise lexical grounding. None of this is visible to the user, since the UI shows no provenance signal beyond an incidental percentage-or-bullet. The good news: the concrete fix for the primary bug (FTS tokenizer choice) is already documented as a first-class option in the very library this app uses (@monlite/fts's `tokenize: "trigram"`), making this a scoped, single-context fix rather than a redesign.
